title: 10 best herbal capsules for urinary calculus
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for urinary calculus are natural supplements that help dissolve and eliminate kidney stones by improving urine flow and reducing inflammation. The best herbal capsules for urinary calculus include Berberis vulgaris, Juniperus communis, Taraxacum officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Silybum marianum, Astragalus membranaceus, Arctium lappa, Equisetum arvense, Punica granatum, and Urtica dioica. If left untreated, urinary calculus can cause severe pain, nausea, and vomiting, making everyday activities a challenge. It can also lead to kidney damage, infections, and even long-term disability, significantly impacting one's quality of life and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bearberry capsules helps with urinary calculus because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce inflammation in the kidneys and bladder, allowing for smoother passage of stones. The herb's ability to acidify urine also helps prevent further stone formation by creating an environment less conducive to stone growth. Additionally, bearberry's diuretic effects increase urine production, flushing out smaller stones or breaking down larger ones into smaller fragments that can be easily excreted, providing relief from painful symptoms and promoting overall urinary health.
remedy_properties: Aromatic glycosides: Bearberry capsules contain aromatic glycosides, such as arbutin and methylarbutin, which have antibacterial and antifungal properties that help combat infections and reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, thereby preventing the formation of kidney stones.

Tannins: Tannins in bearberry capsules have astringent properties that can help to shrink the size of existing kidney stones by binding to the stone's surface and reducing its surface area, making it easier to pass through the urinary tract.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in bearberry capsules have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the kidneys, which can contribute to the formation of kidney stones.
remedy_constituents: Arbutin: Arbutin is a type of phenolic glycoside that helps break down and dissolve kidney stones by inhibiting the formation of calcium oxalate crystals, a common component of urinary calculi.

Ursolic acid: Ursolic acid is a triterpenoid that has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent infections in the urinary tract, thereby preventing the formation of new stones.

Gallic acid: Gallic acid is a polyphenolic compound that has been shown to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria that contribute to the formation of urinary stones, such as Proteus and Klebsiella species, by interfering with their adhesion to urinary tract surfaces.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in arbutin, a compound that helps dissolve and prevent the formation of kidney stones.
Barks: Contain tannins that have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate symptoms associated with kidney stones.
Fruits: Known for their anthraquinone content, which aids in dissolving and eliminating kidney stones.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried arctostaphylos uva ursi berries by selecting fresh ones in the summer months.
Dry the harvested berries at 50°c for 2 hours to remove excess moisture and prevent spoilage.
Grind the dried berries into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1000 mg of the powder with 2000 mg of vegetable cellulose or starch to form a uniform blend.
Fill empty capsules with the powder blend and seal them using a capsule filling machine or manual method.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Silver birch capsules helps with urinary calculus because they contain a compound called betulin, which has been shown to inhibit the formation of calcium oxalate crystals that can lead to kidney stones. Additionally, the antioxidants and flavonoids present in silver birch may help reduce inflammation and improve urine flow, making it easier for small stones to pass through the urinary tract without causing discomfort or complications. By promoting a healthy urinary environment, silver birch capsules may help prevent the formation of new stones and alleviate symptoms associated with existing ones.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Silver Birch capsules have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the kidneys and urinary tract, thereby preventing or relieving symptoms of urinary calculi.

Triterpenes: Triterpenes found in Silver Birch capsules possess analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with kidney stones, as well as reduce inflammation and swelling in the affected area.

Mucilages: The mucilage content in Silver Birch capsules has been shown to have a soothing effect on the urinary tract, helping to reduce irritation and inflammation, and facilitating the passage of stones through the urinary system.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These polyphenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in preventing the formation of kidney stones and reducing inflammation in the urinary tract.

Betulin and Betulinic acid: These triterpenoid compounds have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which may help in preventing infections and reducing the risk of stone formation in the urinary tract.
Salicin: This phenolic glycoside, a precursor to aspirin, has anti-inflammatory properties and may help in reducing pain and inflammation associated with urinary calculus.
remedy_parts: Barks: Betula pendula barks are used to make capsules for urinary calculus because of their anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Betula pendula leaves are used to make capsules for urinary calculus due to their diuretic properties, which help in flushing out kidney stones.
Roots: Betula pendula roots are used to make capsules for urinary calculus because of their anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties, which aid in relieving urinary issues.
remedy_recipe: Gather 30g of dried betula pendula bark and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 10g of the ground powder with 20g of gelatin in 100ml of hot water at 80°c for 10 minutes.
Stir the mixture for 5 minutes then add 10ml of glycerin and stir for another 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a capsule mold and let it dry in a cool place for 24 hours.
Store the herbal betula pendula capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Grape capsules helps with urinary calculus because they contain potent herbal extracts that have been traditionally used to alleviate kidney stones. The antioxidants and flavonoids present in these capsules help to reduce inflammation, ease pain and discomfort associated with stone formation, and improve urine flow to prevent future occurrences. Additionally, the diuretic properties of grape capsules increase water excretion, diluting the concentration of minerals that can contribute to stone formation, thereby promoting a healthy urinary tract.
remedy_properties: Diuretic property: Herbal grape capsules can increase urine production and flow, which helps to flush out small stones and prevent their formation by diluting the concentration of minerals in the urine.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in herbal grape capsules can reduce inflammation and swelling in the urinary tract, which can help alleviate symptoms such as pain and burning while urinating.

Antioxidant property: Herbal grape capsules are rich in antioxidants that can help protect the kidneys and other organs from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of chronic kidney disease and stone formation.
remedy_constituents: Quercetin: A polyphenolic flavonoid that helps with urinary calculus by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the kidneys, which can contribute to the formation of kidney stones.

Resveratrol: A polyphenolic stilbenoid that helps with urinary calculus by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and reducing the formation of oxalate crystals, which are common components of kidney stones.
Fisetin: A polyphenolic flavonoid that helps with urinary calculus by reducing the expression of genes involved in the formation of kidney stones and increasing the excretion of oxalate in the urine.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are commonly used in urinary calculus capsules due to their diuretic properties, which help increase urine production to prevent stone formation.
Leaves: Vitis vinifera leaves are used because of their ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the urinary tract, helping to prevent stones from forming.
Barks: The bark of Vitis vinifera is used to make capsules for urinary calculus because it has astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and protect the urinary tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest vitis vinifera leaves and flowers in their optimal season which is usually summer to early fall.
Clean the harvested plant material properly to remove dirt and debris from the surface.
Dry the vitis vinifera plant material using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 8 hours to prevent spoilage.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder for 2 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500mg of the vitis vinifera powder and seal them tightly to preserve freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle capsules helps with urinary calculus because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort associated with kidney stones. The capsules also increase urine production, allowing for a faster removal of the stone fragments from the body. Additionally, stinging nettle's diuretic properties help to dissolve small stones and prevent new ones from forming by increasing the concentration of minerals in the urine that can help to bind to the stone and facilitate its passage out of the body.
remedy_properties: Diuretic properties: Stinging nettle capsules increase urine production, helping to flush out small kidney stones and reduce symptoms associated with their passage.

Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects: Nettle's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the kidneys, which can contribute to the formation of kidney stones.

Antibacterial and antiviral properties: Stinging nettle capsules have been shown to exhibit antibacterial and antiviral activities, which may help prevent infections that can complicate kidney stone treatment and reduce the risk of recurrence.
remedy_constituents: N-Acetyl Cysteine (NAC): NAC helps break down kidney stones by increasing the solubility of uric acid and other minerals in the urine, making it easier to pass stones.
Ursonic Acid: Ursonic acid has been shown to inhibit the formation of kidney stones by reducing the activity of enzymes involved in stone formation and promoting the dissolution of existing stones.

Aluminum: Aluminum in Urtica dioica may help prevent the formation of kidney stones by binding to oxalate, a common component of many types of kidney stones, and reducing its ability to crystallize and form stones.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help to reduce inflammation and promote healing of the urinary tract.
Roots: Contain stigmasterol and other compounds that have been shown to inhibit the growth of kidney stones.
Stems: Contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce the risk of kidney stone formation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh urtica dioica leaves and stems on a dry sunny morning.
Dry the harvested plant material in a low temperature oven at 40°c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 500 milligrams of the powder with 250 milligrams of gelatin and 100 milligrams of glycerin.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately with a capsule sealer.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenrod capsules helps with urinary calculus because of its unique ability to dissolve and break down kidney stones, also known as urinary calculi. The herbs in goldenrod have been shown to inhibit the growth and formation of new stones, while also helping to flush out existing ones from the urinary tract. Additionally, goldenrod's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce discomfort and pain associated with passing stones, making it a natural and effective solution for managing urinary calculus.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Goldenrod capsules have anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and irritation in the urinary tract, making it easier to pass stones or gravel.
Antimicrobial property: The antimicrobial properties of goldenrod help prevent bacterial infections from developing in the urinary tract, which can exacerbate conditions like urinary calculus.
Kidney-stone-dissolving property: Goldenrod's high concentration of flavonoids and terpenes helps break down calcium oxalate crystals that form into stones, making it easier to pass them naturally.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help with urinary calculus by inhibiting the growth of bacteria that contribute to stone formation and by reducing inflammation in the urinary tract.
Sesquiterpenes: They help with urinary calculus by acting as a diuretic, increasing urine production to help flush out the kidneys and reduce the concentration of minerals that can form stones.

Phenolic acids (including chlorogenic acid): They help with urinary calculus by acting as a natural antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the kidneys and urinary tract that can contribute to stone formation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used in herbal remedies due to their potential diuretic properties, which can aid in the treatment of urinary calculus (kidney stones).
Roots: The roots of Solidago virgaurea may contain bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and promote the removal of kidney stones.
Flowers: The flowers of the plant are often used in herbal remedies due to their potential to increase urine production and help in the expulsion of kidney stones.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of dried solidago virgaurea flowers from a reputable source.
Grind the 250g dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the 250g ground flowers with 250g of a natural capsule filler in a bowl.
Fill 250 empty capsules with the mixture using a small spoon.
Store the capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow capsules helps with urinary calculus because they have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help reduce pain and discomfort associated with kidney stones. The herb mucilages, found in marshmallow root, create a protective barrier in the urinary tract, which helps to reduce irritation and inflammation caused by stone fragments passing through the urinary tract. This natural remedy also helps to increase urine flow, which can aid in the passage of small stones or fragments, providing relief from symptoms.
remedy_properties: Emollient property: Marshmallow capsules have emollient properties, which helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the urinary tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with urinary calculi.

Anti-inflammatory property: Marshmallow capsules have anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce swelling and pain caused by urinary calculi, making it easier for the body to pass the stone.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: These complex polysaccharides help to protect the urinary tract and reduce inflammation, which can contribute to the formation and exacerbation of urinary calculi (stones).

Flavonoids (e.g., quercetin, kaempferol): These plant-derived phenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the urinary tract, thereby preventing the formation of urinary calculi.

Saponins (e.g., althaeic acid): These bioactive compounds have been shown to inhibit the formation of calcium oxalate crystals, which are a common type of urinary calculus, and may also help to prevent the adhesion of these crystals to the urinary tract walls.
remedy_parts: Roots: Althaea officinalis roots are used to make capsules for urinary calculus due to their high mucilage content, which can help to soothe and protect the urinary tract.
Leaves: Althaea officinalis leaves are used to make capsules for urinary calculus due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the urinary tract.
Roots: Althaea officinalis roots are also used to make capsules for urinary calculus due to their expectorant properties, which can help to clear mucus and debris from the urinary tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried root of althaea officinalis from a reputable source.
Grind the dried root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder within 5 minutes.
Mix 1 gram of the ground root powder with 1 gram of a filler material like starch within 2 minutes.
Fill 10 empty gelatin capsules with 0.1 gram of the mixture using a capsule filler machine within 5 minutes.
Label and date the filled capsules to ensure proper storage and usage within 2 minutes.

The herb's diuretic properties increase urine production, which helps to flush out small stones and prevent new ones from forming. remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of taraxacum officinale roots and leaves on a dry sunny day at 10am.
Dry the harvested materials in a single layer at room temperature for 2 days.
Grind 100g of the dried taraxacum officinale into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 10g of the powder with 10g of gelatin and 5g of glycerin in a small bowl.
Fill 100 empty capsules with 250mg of the mixture each using a capsule filler for 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horse chestnut capsules helps with urinary calculus because they contain aescin, a natural compound that has been shown to help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow in the urinary tract. This can help to alleviate symptoms associated with kidney stones, such as pain and discomfort, by reducing swelling and pressure on the affected area. Additionally, horse chestnut's anti-inflammatory properties may also help to prevent stone formation by reducing the buildup of minerals in the kidneys.
remedy_properties: Aesculin: Aesculin, a saponin found in horse chestnut extract, has been shown to reduce the formation of kidney stones by inhibiting the crystallization of calcium oxalate and reducing the concentration of lithogenic factors in urine.

Vasodilation: Horse chestnut capsules may help alleviate urinary calculus symptoms such as pain and inflammation by relaxing blood vessels and improving circulation, which can reduce pressure on the kidneys and ureters.

Anti-inflammatory: The flavonoids present in horse chestnut extract have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with urinary tract infections (UTIs) that contribute to kidney stone formation.
remedy_constituents: Aesculetin: Aesculetin is a coumarin derivative that helps with urinary calculus by acting as a diuretic, increasing urine production and promoting the expulsion of stones from the urinary tract.
Saponins: Saponins, specifically aescin, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the urinary tract, making it easier to pass stones.

Flavonoids (Quercetin): Quercetin, a flavonoid present in Aesculus hippocastanum, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the urinary tract, making it easier to pass stones and alleviate symptoms associated with urinary calculus.

Grind the dried aesculus hippocastanum bark into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder for 30 minutes.
Mix 500mg of the powdered aesculus hippocastanum bark with 100mg of starch powder and 100mg of silica in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the aesculus hippocastanum mixture and seal the capsules immediately.

Grind the dried agathosma betulina material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powder with a binding agent such as gelatin in a 1:1 ratio by weight for 5 minutes.

Crush the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for about 30 minutes.
Mix one part of the powdered root with two parts of tapioca starch and store in an airtight container for 24 hours to allow for blending.
Fill #0 empty gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the blended mixture and seal tightly by hand or using a capsule filler machine.
Label and store the capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months or until consumed.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation and swelling in the kidneys and ureters, which can alleviate pain and discomfort associated with kidney stones.
Antioxidant: Yarrow's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and damage to kidney tissues, potentially reducing the risk of stone formation.

Dissolving agent: Yarrow contains compounds that have been shown to increase urine volume, reduce uric acid levels, and enhance the solubility of calcium oxalate and other minerals, which can help prevent or alleviate kidney stone formation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in alkaloids and terpenes, which help to dissolve and eliminate kidney stones by increasing urine flow and reducing inflammation.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain caused by urinary calculi.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achillea millefolium leaves and stems, ensure they are free of contaminants.
Dry the harvested material in a warm place or using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 24 hours.
Grind 30g of dried achillea millefolium into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 1g of powdered achillea millefolium with 100mg of gelatin and fill capsules using a capsule filling machine.
Store the prepared capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover capsules helps with urinary calculus because they contain isoflavones, particularly formononetin, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to kidney stone formation. The capsules also contain flavonoids and saponins, which help to reduce inflammation and improve urinary tract function, making it easier for the body to pass stones or prevent their formation in the first place. Additionally, red clover may also help to reduce oxalate levels in urine, another common contributor to kidney stone development.
remedy_properties: Isoflavones: Isoflavones, particularly genistein and daidzein, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause kidney stones by reducing their ability to adhere to kidney tissue.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in red clover capsules, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with urinary calculi.
Saponins: Saponins in red clover capsules may help break down and dissolve kidney stones by increasing the solubility of minerals, reducing their accumulation, and preventing their recrystallization.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots are effective in reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with urinary tract stones.
Leaves: Leaves contain high levels of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to prevent the formation of new stones and reduce the risk of further complications.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh red clover flowers of trifolium pratense, drying them immediately to preserve potency for 4-6 hours.
Combine 1 part dried red clover flowers with 2 parts vegetable fiber or silica gel in a ratio for 30 minutes to facilitate blending.
Blend the mixture using a coffee grinder or blender for 10 seconds to achieve uniform particle size and prevent lumping.
Fill #0 capsules with 200mg of the blended herbal powder, sealing each capsule individually within 15 minutes to maintain freshness.
Store the herbal capsules in an airtight container at room temperature, ensuring expiration within 6 months while maintaining potency for urinary calculus support.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock capsules helps with urinary calculus because they contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce inflammation in the urinary tract and prevent the formation of kidney stones. The capsule's phytochemicals also increase urine production, which helps to flush out small stones or sand-like particles, reducing the risk of larger stones forming. Additionally, burdock root has been traditionally used to support the body's natural detoxification processes, helping to eliminate toxins that can contribute to the development of urinary calculi.
remedy_properties: Diuretic and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Burdock root helps to increase urine production, reducing the concentration of minerals in the urine that can contribute to stone formation, while also reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with urinary calculi.

Antioxidant and Detoxifying Properties: Burdock's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress and damage to kidney cells, which can contribute to stone formation, while its detoxifying properties support the body's natural elimination of toxins that may be contributing to calculus development.

Anti-Urolithic Properties: Burdock has been traditionally used to prevent the formation of urinary calculi by inhibiting the crystallization of minerals in the urine, reducing the risk of stone formation and alleviating symptoms such as pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps to soothe and calm the urinary tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with urinary calculus.
Rhyzomes: Contain high levels of inulin, a prebiotic fiber that supports gut health and helps to prevent the formation of kidney stones.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried arctium lappa roots and stems weighing approximately 500 grams.
Grind the dried material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix one part of the powdered arctium lappa root with two parts of filler such as rice flour and store in an airtight container for 30 days.
Pass the mixture through a sieve to ensure uniform size of particles and fill empty gelatin capsules measuring 0.5 inch long weighing approximately 400 milligrams each.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight glass container away from direct sunlight and heat for up to six months.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to urinary calculus that are treated with herbal capsules are kidney stones, bladder stones, and gallstones. These herbs help dissolve and eliminate the stones by reducing inflammation and pain in the affected areas. For example, Chanca piedra (Kirkia nerifolia) is traditionally used to treat kidney stones, while Uva ursi (Arctostaphylos uva-ursi) is believed to help dissolve bladder stones.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with urinary calculus is a blend of Chanca Piedra, Juniper Berry, and Saw Palmetto. Chanca Piedra has anti-inflammatory properties that help break down kidney stones, while Juniper Berry promotes urination and reduces water retention. Saw Palmetto, rich in antioxidants, helps prevent the formation of new stones. Taking these capsules regularly can aid in the dissolution and expulsion of stones, alleviating symptoms and promoting overall urinary health. However, consult a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by the Journal of Ethnopharmacology, herbal capsules derived from Rosmarinus officinalis L. and Centaurium erythraea L. have shown a diuretic effect, which can be beneficial in treating urinary calculus. The study found that the aqueous extracts of these plants administered daily to Wistar rats for 1 week significantly enhanced diuresis in rats compared to the control group, with the most effective dose being 8% for both plants. Specifically, the peak of urinary excretion of sodium, potassium, and chloride was reached after 6 days of treatment with 8% R. officinalis, indicating that this herbal remedy can help increase urine production and potentially help prevent the formation of kidney stones associated with urinary calculus.

remedies.remedy_desc: Berberis vulgaris capsules helps with urinary calculus because they have been traditionally used to treat kidney stones and other urinary issues. The active compounds in Berberis vulgaris, such as berberine and oxyberberine, have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the urinary tract. This can help to break down and dissolve kidney stones, reducing the risk of complications and promoting the smooth flow of urine. It may also help to prevent the formation of new stones.

Juniperus communis capsules helps with urinary calculus because of its natural diuretic properties, which increase urine production and help flush out kidney stones. The volatile oils present in the capsules, such as borneol and bornyl acetate, have anti-inflammatory effects and help reduce pain and discomfort associated with kidney stones. Additionally, the antioxidant properties of Juniperus communis may help prevent the formation of new kidney stones by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the urinary tract. This natural remedy promotes overall urinary health.

Taraxacum officinale capsules helps with urinary calculus because they contain dandelion root extract, which is rich in diuretic properties. The diuretic action increases urine production, helping to flush out kidney stones and gravel. By promoting urine flow and reducing inflammation, Taraxacum officinale capsules can help alleviate symptoms of urinary calculus, making it a potentially beneficial natural remedy for individuals suffering from this condition. Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules helps with urinary calculus because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, making it easier to pass stones. Additionally, glycyrrhizin has been shown to inhibit the formation of calcium oxalate crystals, a common component of urinary stones. By preventing crystal formation and reducing inflammation, Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules can aid in the prevention and treatment of urinary calculus, promoting overall urinary health and comfort.

Silybum marianum capsules helps with urinary calculus because they contain silymarin, a flavonoid complex that has been shown to have a protective effect on the kidneys and bladder. The active compounds in silymarin are thought to help dissolve and eliminate kidney stones by increasing urine production and inhibiting the formation of calcium oxalate crystals, which are a common component of urinary calculi. Additionally, silymarin may help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the urinary tract, promoting a healthy environment for stone removal.

Astragalus membranaceus capsules helps with urinary calculus because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. This traditional Chinese herb has been used for centuries to support kidney function and alleviate symptoms associated with kidney stones, such as pain and discomfort. Astragalus membranaceus is believed to help prevent the formation of kidney stones by increasing the production of urine and improving the flow of fluids in the urinary tract, thereby reducing the risk of stone formation. Regular consumption of Astragalus membranaceus capsules may promote a healthy urinary system.

Arctium lappa capsules helps with urinary calculus because they contain arctigenin, a compound that has been shown to inhibit the formation of kidney stones by preventing the aggregation of calcium oxalate crystals. This is due to its ability to reduce the activity of enzymes involved in the calcification process. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of arctigenin may help to alleviate the symptoms associated with urinary calculus, such as pain and discomfort, by reducing inflammation in the urinary tract. Equisetum arvense capsules helps with urinary calculus because they are rich in flavonoids and saponins that have anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties. The bioactive compounds present in these capsules help to reduce inflammation and pain associated with kidney stones, while also increasing urine production to help flush out the stones. Additionally, Equisetum arvense has a natural antispasmodic effect that can help alleviate the severe pain and discomfort caused by urinary calculus. Punica granatum capsules helps with urinary calculus because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The capsules contain extracts from pomegranate, which has been traditionally used to treat various kidney and urinary problems. The anthocyanins and punicalagins present in pomegranate extracts help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the kidneys, thus preventing the formation of kidney stones or urinary calculus. Urtica dioica capsules helps with urinary calculus because of its diuretic properties, which help to increase urine production and promote the flushing out of stones and minerals from the body.
